source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

segments.text: My project is on the 19th-century British architect George Dodds, who used to be famous not just in the northeast of the UK but throughout the country.
segments.translation: 我的项目研究的是19世纪英国建筑师乔治·多兹，他曾经不仅在英国东北部，而且在全国都颇有名气。

segments.text: At first, I chose him for my term project because, in my early teens, I had always admired Mitten Hall, a mansion he built near my home.
segments.translation: 起初，我之所以选择他作为学期项目的研究对象，是因为我十几岁出头时一直很欣赏他在我家附近建造的米滕庄园。

segments.text: Later, I wanted to learn more about the man responsible for it.
segments.translation: 后来，我想进一步了解这座建筑背后的设计者。

segments.text: I thought I'd use the project as an excuse and managed to unearth a wealth of information about architecture and fascinating life.
segments.translation: 我想借这个项目为由深入研究，并成功发掘了大量有关建筑和他精彩人生的资料。

segments.text: I thought I'd start today by telling you a little about Dodds himself before looking at a selection of his more substantial buildings.
segments.translation: 今天，我想先向大家简单介绍一下多兹本人，然后再探讨他的几座较为重要的建筑。

segments.text: He was the son of a landscape gardener and had a brief but successful career in his father's business.
segments.translation: 他是一名景观园艺师的儿子，曾短暂从事父亲的事业，并且颇为成功。

segments.text: But the young Dodds started to resent what he saw as his father's old-fashioned approach to landscape.
segments.translation: 但年轻的多兹开始反感父亲在景观设计方面被他视为守旧的做法。

segments.text: Dodds began to spend a lot of time thinking about how he could break with convention, creating buildings in a new relationship with their surroundings.
segments.translation: 多兹开始花大量时间思考如何打破传统，让建筑与周围环境形成一种新的关系。

segments.text: In 1810, Dodds, aged 23 at the time, decided to go to London to study art under the eminent watercolourist Jack Vamos.
segments.translation: 1810年，当时23岁的多兹决定前往伦敦，师从著名水彩画家杰克·瓦莫斯学习艺术。

segments.text: His friends strongly encouraged him to stay and work in London as architects were highly regarded there.
segments.translation: 他的朋友们极力鼓励他留在伦敦工作，因为建筑师在那里很受尊重。

segments.text: But by 1811, he decided to move back to northeast England because he discovered that more clients would hire him for his fashionable ideas from London.
segments.translation: 但到1811年，他决定搬回英格兰东北部，因为他发现，凭借从伦敦带回的时尚理念，会有更多客户聘请他。

segments.text: Having a large customer base, the city council included, Dodds was able to set up an architectural firm of his own.
segments.translation: 由于拥有庞大的客户群，其中包括市议会，多兹得以创办自己的建筑事务所。

segments.text: Dodds distanced himself from other builder-architects and gradually established himself as one of the new breed of professional architects.
segments.translation: 多兹让自己有别于其他兼做建筑师的营造商，并逐渐成为新一代专业建筑师中的一员。

segments.text: And one of his signature designs was the Royal Arcade, which was commissioned by the city council.
segments.translation: 他的标志性设计之一是受市议会委托建造的皇家拱廊。

segments.text: Although never practised in London, Dodds gained a national reputation when he joined the newly-formed Northern Architectural Association and was elected to be its first chairman.
segments.translation: 尽管从未在伦敦执业，多兹加入新成立的北方建筑协会并当选首任主席后，还是在全国赢得了声誉。

segments.text: He retired from active work in his 70s after suffering a stroke, from which he never fully recovered.
segments.translation: 他70多岁时中风，此后再也没有完全康复，于是退出一线工作。

segments.text: Dodds died at the age of 77 at his home.
segments.translation: 多兹在家中去世，享年77岁。

segments.text: Dodds was versatile and could build houses of many different styles that his clients desired.
segments.translation: 多兹才华全面，能够按照客户的要求建造多种不同风格的住宅。

segments.text: His country houses are too little known, mainly because they are not so large that they are open to the public and are often still in private hands, hidden behind parkland and trees.
segments.translation: 他的乡间宅邸鲜为人知，主要是因为它们规模不够大，无法向公众开放，而且往往仍归私人所有，掩映在园地和树林之后。

segments.text: One was Mitten Hall, built in 1824 and finished two years later.
segments.translation: 米滕庄园就是其中之一，始建于1824年，两年后竣工。

segments.text: The outstanding characteristics of Mitten Hall are Dodds' use of beautiful golden sandstone, Grecian styled entrance porticos, elegant staircases leading to an upper gallery.
segments.translation: 米滕庄园最突出的特色包括多兹采用的美丽金色砂岩、希腊风格的入口门廊，以及通往楼上回廊的雅致楼梯。

segments.text: The building has a domed ceiling with a glass centrepiece.
segments.translation: 这座建筑有一个穹顶，中央镶有玻璃装饰。

segments.text: Even though the quality of the stonework was superb, some of the windows seemed to leak on rainy days.
segments.translation: 尽管石工质量极佳，但有些窗户似乎会在雨天漏水。

segments.text: The building was renovated in 1987 and is now served as a conference centre.
segments.translation: 这座建筑于1987年翻修，如今用作会议中心。

segments.text: In 1830, the town council decided to build Royal Arcade on a site that used to be a corn exchange market and entrusted George Dodds with the design.
segments.translation: 1830年，镇议会决定在原谷物交易市场的旧址上建造皇家拱廊，并委托乔治·多兹负责设计。

segments.text: And the shopping arcade was completed by 1832.
segments.translation: 这条购物拱廊于1832年建成。

segments.text: The whole design was intended as an elegant shopping experience and was renowned for its grand balcony.
segments.translation: 整个设计旨在营造优雅的购物体验，并以其宏伟的阳台闻名。

segments.text: Unfortunately, the Royal Arcade was not a commercial success, as it was positioned too far from the town centre, causing great difficulty in delivery.
segments.translation: 遗憾的是，皇家拱廊在商业上并不成功，因为它离市中心太远，导致送货非常困难。

segments.text: The arcade was demolished as early as the 1880s and is now used as a car park for neighbouring shops today.
segments.translation: 这条拱廊早在19世纪80年代就被拆除，如今其旧址用作附近商店的停车场。

segments.text: The final building I want to show you is Morton Gaol which Dodds completed in 1839.
segments.translation: 我想向大家展示的最后一座建筑是莫顿监狱，多兹于1839年将其建成。

segments.text: It is a sturdy building with impressive towers based on medieval castles.
segments.translation: 这是一座坚固的建筑，拥有以中世纪城堡为蓝本的宏伟塔楼。

segments.text: However, although beautiful, the gaol wasn't 100% functional.
segments.translation: 然而，这座监狱虽然美观，功能上却并非尽善尽美。

segments.text: More striking was the absence of any space for exercise for the prisoners.
segments.translation: 更引人注目的问题是，监狱里完全没有供囚犯活动的空间。

segments.text: An extra courtyard had to be added for this at a later date.
segments.translation: 因此，后来不得不增建一个庭院。

segments.text: Morton ceased to be a penal institution in the 1940s, when it was scheduled for demolition, but was purchased for use as a hotel.
segments.translation: 20世纪40年代，莫顿不再用作刑罚机构，当时原计划将其拆除，但后来有人买下它，打算改建成酒店。

segments.text: That never came to pass.
segments.translation: 但这个计划始终没有实现。

segments.text: And as many of you will know, it is now run as a Transport Museum with a whole collection of vehicles that tell the city's story through transport.
segments.translation: 正如你们许多人所知道的那样，它如今作为交通博物馆运营，馆内收藏了各种车辆，通过交通工具讲述这座城市的历史。
